text: BREAKING: Trump just set an April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz or face destruction of all energy infrastructure including Kharg Island oil facilities. This is the biggest escalation threat since Day 1. Markets reacting — Brent jumped 5% to $106.
verificationNote: CNBC, Al Jazeera confirmed Trump address and deadline.

text: IDF claims 400+ strikes on Iran in 48 hours using 650+ munitions. 20 weapons production sites in Tehran hit. Isfahan bunker-busters caused massive secondary explosions — largest of the war. Steel plants struck in Isfahan and Ahvaz.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera Day 33 liveblog, Alma Research Center confirmed.

text: Vafaei elimination confirmed: IRGC Quds Force engineering chief who built Hezbollah's tunnel network for 20 years killed in Mahallat. Also: Hezbollah's Southern Front Commander 'Hajj Sadiq' eliminated by Israeli Navy. Two high-value targets in 24 hours.
verificationNote: Ynet News, JNS, Legal Insurrection, Alma Center confirmed both kills.

text: Hezbollah doubled attack waves: 46 waves on March 31 (29 rockets/missiles + 17 UAVs). Iran fired 10 ballistic missiles at central Israel during Passover Seder. Coordinated triple-front attack: Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis. 14 wounded including 11-year-old girl.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, Alma Center confirmed.

text: Economic warfare escalating: Iran's internet at 4% of normal. 35-nation Hormuz conference in London tomorrow. UAE preparing to help open Hormuz by force (WSJ). China-Pakistan 5-point peace plan proposed. Australia halves fuel excise. Oil volatile $101-$106.

Day 33 night (8:20 PM PDT): Trump address — 2-3 weeks, April 6 deadline; Netanyahu 'Ten Plagues'; Iran denies ceasefire; Brent $106/WTI $104; Vafaei + Hajj Sadiq killed; 46 Hezbollah waves; IDF 400+ strikes in 48h; Isfahan bunker-busters; Kuwait airport hit; gas $4.06; UK Hormuz conf tomorrow.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, AAA, Ynet, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Alma Center.

text: IRGC just threatened 18 major US tech companies (Apple, Google, Meta, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing) — ordered employee evacuations. Markets rattled. This is unprecedented: first time a state actor has directly threatened named corporations as military targets.
verificationNote: CNBC and Time confirmed IRGC statement.

text: 4 Iranian missile salvos overnight — cluster warheads again. 2 babies among 4 wounded in Bnei Brak. Meanwhile Hezbollah launched 50+ rockets at northern Israel on Passover morning. Coordinated timing continues: Iran + Hezbollah striking simultaneously.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog and IDF confirmed.

text: Oil +8% this morning — Brent $109.16, WTI $108.36. Complete reversal of yesterday's peace-hope dip to $101. Trump's 'hit extremely hard' vow + April 6 Hormuz deadline = markets pricing in escalation, not peace. Goldman now sees $110 average through April.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed price data, Goldman forecast per EIA STEO.

text: UK 35-nation Hormuz conference starting now — US conspicuously absent. FM Cooper chairing. France, Germany, Italy, Canada, UAE, Japan all present. This could be the beginning of a non-US Hormuz reopening framework. Military planning session to follow.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and Asia Times confirmed summit.

text: China-Pakistan 5-point peace plan is the first credible ceasefire framework from a major power. Key: ceasefire + Hormuz reopening packaged together. Pakistan serving as US-Iran mediator. Axios says US may not oppose behind scenes. Watch for Iran's response.
verificationNote: Middle East Eye, Axios, SCMP confirmed plan details.

text: Day 34 AM (4:40 AM PDT): Brent $109/WTI $108 (+8%); 4 Iran salvos overnight (cluster, 4 hurt); Hezbollah 50+ rockets; IRGC threatens 18 tech giants; UK 35-nation Hormuz summit; China-Pakistan 5-pt peace plan; Rubio-Hegseth ground troop feud; Argentina IRGC designation; gas $4.064.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Times of Israel, AAA, Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye.
